{\"aggregated_output\": \"from __future__ import annotations\\n\\nimport logging\\nfrom contextlib import asynccontextmanager\\nfrom typing import AsyncIterator\\n\\nfrom fastapi import FastAPI\\nfrom fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware\\n\\nfrom app.api.router import router as api_router\\nfrom app.api.v1.router import router as api_v1_router\\nfrom app.settings import AppSettings, get_settings\\n\\n\\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\\n\\n\\ndef configure_logging(settings: AppSettings) -> None:\\n    logging.basicConfig(\\n        level=getattr(logging, settings.log_level, logging.INFO),\\n        format=\\\"%(levelname)s %(name)s %(message)s\\\",\\n    )\\n\\n\\n@asynccontextmanager\\nasync def lifespan(app: FastAPI) -> AsyncIterator[None]:\\n    settings = get_settings()\\n    configure_logging(settings)\\n    app.state.settings = settings\\n\\n    logger.info(\\n        \\\"Starting %s in %s mode on %s:%s\\\",\\n        settings.app_name,\\n        settings.environment,\\n        settings.host,\\n        settings.port,\\n    )\\n\\n    yield\\n\\n    logger.info(\\\"Stopping %s\\\", settings.app_name)\\n\\n\\ndef create_app() -> FastAPI:\\n    settings = get_settings()\\n\\n    app = FastAPI(\\n        title=settings.app_name,\\n        version=settings.version,\\n        lifespan=lifespan,\\n    )\\n\\n    app.add_middleware(\\n        CORSMiddleware,\\n        allow_origins=list(settings.cors_allowed_origins),\\n        allow_credentials=True,\\n        allow_methods=[\\\"*\\\"],\\n        allow_headers=[\\\"*\\\"],\\n    )\\n\\n    app.include_router(api_router)\\n    app.include_router(api_v1_router, prefix=settings.api_v1_prefix)\\n\\n    return app\\n\\n\\napp = create_app()\\n\", \"command\": \"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n {\"aggregated_output\": \"from __future__ import annotations\\n\\nimport os\\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\\nfrom functools import lru_cache\\nfrom typing import Tuple\\n\\n\\nDEFAULT_CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS = (\\n    \\\"http://localhost:8566\\\",\\n    \\\"http://127.0.0.1:8566\\\",\\n)\\n\\n\\ndef _read_bool(name: str, default: bool) -> bool:\\n    value = os.getenv(name)\\n\\n    if value is None:\\n        return default\\n\\n    return value.strip().lower() in {\\\"1\\\", \\\"true\\\", \\\"yes\\\", \\\"on\\\"}\\n\\n\\ndef _read_int(name: str, default: int) -> int:\\n    value = os.getenv(name)\\n\\n    if value is None:\\n        return default\\n\\n    return int(value)\\n\\n\\ndef _read_csv(name: str, default: Tuple[str, ...]) -> Tuple[str, ...]:\\n    value = os.getenv(name)\\n\\n    if not value:\\n        return default\\n\\n    items = tuple(item.strip() for item in value.split(\\\",\\\") if item.strip())\\n    return items or default\\n\\n\\ndef _normalize_api_prefix(value: str) -> str:\\n    prefix = value.strip() or \\\"/api/v1\\\"\\n    return prefix if prefix.startswith(\\\"/\\\") else f\\\"/{prefix}\\\"\\n\\n\\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\\nclass AppSettings:\\n    app_name: str\\n    environment: str\\n    version: str\\n    host: str\\n    port: int\\n    reload: bool\\n    api_v1_prefix: str\\n    cors_allowed_origins: Tuple[str, ...]\\n    database_url: str\\n    log_level: str\\n\\n\\n@lru_cache(maxsize=1)\\ndef get_settings() -> AppSettings:\\n    environment = os.getenv(\\\"STORYTELLER_ENVIRONMENT\\\", \\\"development\\\").strip()\\n\\n    return AppSettings(\\n        app_name=os.getenv(\\\"STORYTELLER_APP_NAME\\\", \\\"Storyteller API\\\").strip(),\\n        environment=environment,\\n        version=os.getenv(\\\"STORYTELLER_VERSION\\\", \\\"0.1.0\\\").strip(),\\n        host=os.getenv(\\\"STORYTELLER_HOST\\\", \\\"0.0.0.0\\\").strip(),\\n        port=_read_int(\\\"STORYTELLER_PORT\\\", 8565),\\n        reload=_read_bool(\\n            \\\"STORYTELLER_RELOAD\\\",\\n            environment.lower() == \\\"development\\\",\\n        ),\\n        api_v1_prefix=_normalize_api_prefix(\\n            os.getenv(\\\"STORYTELLER_API_V1_PREFIX\\\", \\\"/api/v1\\\"),\\n        ),\\n        cors_allowed_origins=_read_csv(\\n            \\\"STORYTELLER_CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS\\\",\\n            DEFAULT_CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS,\\n        ),\\n        database_url=os.getenv(\\\"STORYTELLER_DATABASE_URL\\\", \\\"\\\").strip(),\\n        log_level=os.getenv(\\\"STORYTELLER_LOG_LEVEL\\\", \\\"INFO\\\").strip().upper(),\\n    )\\n\", \"command\": \"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,260p' backend/app/settings/config.py\\\"\", \"exit_code\": 0, t-scaffold.codex.jsonlines\\nprompts/23-frontend-state-foundation.md\\nprompts/97-security-review.md\\nprompts/12-seed-genres-and-tones.md\\nprompts/81-docx-export-pipeline.md\\nprompts/87-developer-debug-inspector.md\\nprompts/25-chat-window-foundation.md\\nprompts/01-monorepo-skeleton.codex.jsonlines\\nprompts/60-composition-job-orchestration.md\\nprompts/96-compose-dev-and-prod-shapes.md\\nprompts/49-beat-sheet-refinement-ui-and-chat.md\\nprompts/93-accessibility-pass-and-keyboard-support.md\\nprompts/15-event-log-and-audit-trail.md\\nprompts/47-character-refinement-via-chat.md\\nprompts/16-asset-metadata-and-file-records.md\\nprompts/02-vite-react-scaffold.yolopilot.md\\nprompts/76-audio-preview-and-segment-status-ui.md\\nprompts/53-outline-drill-down.md\\nprompts/66-rewrite-prior-segments.md\\nprompts/18-postgres-job-runner-skeleton.md\\nprompts/43-brief-normalization-service.md\\nprompts/base_prompt.md\\nprompts/50-story-length-duration-and-chapter-setup.md\\nprompts/86-completed-session-summary-and-search.md\\nprompts/82-html-markdown-and-reader-formatting.md\\nprompts/77-final-audio-assembly.md\\nprompts/79-audio-pipeline-tests.md\\nprompts/34-ui-events-to-agent-context.md\\nprompts/89-full-journey-e2e-test.md\\nprompts/57-composition-prompt-assembly.md\\nprompts/39-bridge-and-replay-tests.md\\nprompts/03-fastapi-scaffold.md\\nprompts/31-intent-parser-service.md\\nprompts/75-audio-job-orchestration.md\\nprompts/02-vite-react-scaffold.yolopilot.jsonlines\\nprompts/00-project-charter.yolopilot.md\\nprompts/85-audio-player-with-text-sync-hooks.md\\nprompts/00-project-charter.summary.md\\nprompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.md\\nprompts/33-action-echoes-in-chat.md\\nprompts/08-repo-quality-tooling.md\\nprompts/19-data-layer-integration-tests.md\\nprompts/73-gemini-tts-integration.md\\nprompts/71-audio-length-estimation.md\\nprompts/22-workspace-two-pane-layout.md\\nprompts/38-session-replay-and-resume-hydration.md\\nprompts/10-domain-model-and-state-machine.md\\nprompts/40-genre-selection-api-and-ui.md\\nprompts/70-audio-settings-model-and-ui.md\\nprompts/69-composition-loop-e2e-test.md\\nprompts/00-project-charter.yolopilot.jsonlines\\nprompts/27-reusable-form-and-card-components.md\\nprompts/98-demo-data-and-onboarding.md\\nprompts/21-past-sessions-home-screen.md\\nprompts/51-correlation-heuristics.md\\nprompts/35-chat-commands-and-quick-actions.md\\nprompts/55-continuity-bible.md\\nprompts/28-error-loading-and-feedback-states.md\\nprompts/32-ui-action-policy-engine.md\\nprompts/94-performance-pass.md\\nprompts/29-frontend-websocket-client-skeleton.md\\nprompts/58-usage-cost-and-latency-metrics.md\\nprompts/68-autosave-drafts-and-partials.md\\nprompts/09-initial-system-diagram-and-adr.md\\nprompts/91-rate-limits-retries-and-fallbacks.md\\nprompts/24-design-tokens-and-accessibility-foundation.md\\nprompts/84-reader-ui-with-navigation.md\\nprompts/59-planning-funnel-e2e-test.md\\nscripts/README.md\\nREADME.md\\ndocs/README.md\\ndocs/adr/README.md\\nbackend/README.md\\nbackend/requirements.txt\\nbackend/app/worker/README.md\\nbackend/migrations/README.md\\nfrontend/package.json\\nfrontend/README.md\\ntools/README.md\\ntools/webapp-qa/package.json\\ninfra/persistence/README.md\\ninfra/README.md\\ninfra/compose/docker-compose.yml\\ninfra/compose/README.md\\ntest-assets/README.md\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\nprompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.yolopilot.md:839:    prompts/02-vite-react-scaffold.codex.jsonlines:22:{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_10\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,260p' prompts/base_prompt.md\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"# Base Prompt — Build the Bedtime Story + Audio App\\n\\nYou are building a production-minded local-first application that helps a user create a bedtime story from idea to finished text and finished narration audio.\\n\\nThis prompt pack is designed to be used **sequentially**. Read this file first, then work through the numbered prompts in order. Each numbered prompt should be treated as an incremental implementation task inside the same repository. Preserve prior work unless the new prompt explicitly asks you to refactor it.\\n\\n## Product Summary\\n\\nThe product is a guided story-creation studio with a persistent session model. A user should be able to:\\n\\n1. See past story sessions first, including both in-progress and completed work.\\n2. Open a session to resume, edit, or finalize it.\\n3. Use a **two-pane workspace**:\\n   - **Left pane:** chat window, about one-third of the desktop width.\\n   - **Main pane:** structured workflow UI, about two-thirds of the desktop width.\\n4. Control the product in **both directions**:\\n   - The user can type in chat, and the system can translate those messages into UI actions.\\n   - The user can act directly in the UI, and those actions should be reflected in the chat log and in durable session state.\\n5. Move through a staged workflow:\\n   - Genre selection\\n   - Tone selection\\n   - Story setup / free-form brief\\n   - Story pitches\\n   - Character sheet\\n   - Save-the-Cat beat sheet\\n   - Story setup preferences such as word count, runtime, and chapters\\n   - Composition\\n   - Audio configuration and audio generation\\n   - Finalize / read / listen / download\\n6. Download the final story as a **Word document** and the final narration as an audio file.\\n7. Read or listen inside the product UI after generation is complete.\\n\\n## Story-Generation Philosophy\\n\\nThe app is for **bedtime stories**, not generic fiction generation. That means:\\n\\n- Wonder is welcome.\\n- Adventure is welcome.\\n- Mystery is welcome.\\n- Tension is allowed, but it should be modulated for bedtime use.\\n- Emotional repair matters.\\n- The ending should feel safe, satisfying, and restful.\\n- The narration and UI tone should remain calm, readable, and trustworthy.\\n\\nUse the research docs in this prompt pack as direct product input, especially the Save The Cat notes and the curated genre/tone catalog.\\n\\n## Required Story Workflow\\n\\nThe session should support this exact conceptual flow:\\n\\n1. **Genre Selection**\\n   - The user selects from the curated genre list.\\n2. **Tone Selection**\\n   - The user is shown preconfigured tone options for the chosen genre.\\n3. **Story Setup**\\n   - The user provides a free-form description of the story they want to tell.\\n4. **Story Pitches**\\n   - Gemini generates multiple candidate pitches.\\n   - The user can select one, ask for alternatives, or refine via chat.\\n5. **Character Sheet**\\n   - Gemini generates multiple candidate character sheets.\\n   - The user can select or refine via chat.\\n6. **Story Beats**\\n   - Gemini generates a Save-the-Cat beat sheet for the selected plan.\\n   - The user can refine the beats via chat or UI.\\n7. **Story Setup Preferences**\\n   - The user can set soft planning targets such as word count, read-aloud duration, and chapter organization.\\n   - These are **guides**, not strict constraints.\\n8. **Composition**\\n   - The system writes in segments.\\n   - The UI shows a live typewriter-like experience.\\n   - Writing should generally appear faster than the user can comfortably read.\\n   - A visible progress bar should show overall story progress.\\n   - The agent should periodically post chat summaries of what it is writing.\\n   - The user can interrupt and redirect composition, including causing rewrites of prior segments.\\n9. **Audio**\\n   - The user configures voice, music, speed, and related settings.\\n   - The system estimates final length heuristically.\\n   - Audio is generated in segments with visible progress.\\n10. **Finalize**\\n   - The user can read and listen in the UI.\\n   - The user can download the story as a Word document and the final audio as a file.\\n\\n## Non-Negotiable Technical Requirements\\n\\n### Frontend\\n- React\\n- Vite\\n- TypeScript\\n- The browser must **never** hold provider secrets\\n- Chat pane on the left, main pane on the right\\n- Past sessions screen must be the first meaningful screen\\n\\n### Backend\\n- Python\\n- FastAPI\\n- Durable backend-owned business logic\\n- Clear separation between route handlers, domain services, AI orchestration, and persistence\\n\\n### Data and Infrastructure\\n- PostgreSQL is the durable store for structured application state\\n- Use a **file-backed GCS emulator** for object/blob storage in local development\\n- The app must run with Docker Compose\\n- Postgres and object storage must both be **persistent across restarts**\\n- `secrets.yaml` exists locally for API keys and must **not** be committed to git\\n\\n### AI\\n- Use the **Gemini 3.1 family** for text/planning/composition workloads\\n- Keep model IDs centralized in config, not scattered through the codebase\\n- Put AI calls behind backend services and adapters\\n- Use structured outputs wherever determinism matters\\n- Use separate adapters for:\\n  - planning/classification/intent parsing\\n  - long-form story generation\\n  - narration / TTS\\n- Keep narration generation isolated behind a provider-style interface because model offerings evolve\\n\\n## Recommended Architecture\\n\\nUse a calm, maintainable architecture rather than a clever one.\\n\\n### Suggested application shape\\n- `frontend/` — React + Vite app\\n- `backend/` — FastAPI app plus domain services\\n- `infra/` — Docker Compose, Dockerfiles, local infrastructure helpers\\n- `docs/` — architecture notes, ADRs, product docs\\n\\n### Suggested backend layers\\n- `api/` or route handlers\\n- `settings/`\\n- `db/`\\n- `models/`\\n- `repositories/`\\n- `services/`\\n- `worker/`\\n- `ai/`\\n- `storage/`\\n\\n### Suggested durable concepts\\n- Session\\n- Workflow stage\\n- Event log\\n- Selected genre\\n- Selected tone\\n- Story brief\\n- Pitch batch + selected pitch\\n- Character batch + selected character sheet\\n- Beat sheet\\n- Story setup preferences\\n- Outline / chapter or scene plan\\n- Continuity bible\\n- Composition job\\n- Composition segment\\n- Audio job\\n- Asset record\\n\\n## Real-Time and Long-Running Work\\n\\nTreat composition and audio generation as **durable background jobs**, not request-thread work.\\n\\n- The browser should be able to refresh without losing the job.\\n- The UI should receive live progress updates.\\n- The backend should own current truth about progress.\\n- A separate worker process is strongly preferred.\\n- Use PostgreSQL-backed durable job records.\\n- Persist partial outputs frequently enough that resume is trustworthy.\\n\\n## Chat and UI Bridge Rules\\n\\nThe product must support true bidirectional interaction.\\n\\n### Chat-to-UI\\nA user message can propose actions like:\\n- select a tone\\n- regenerate pitches\\n- choose a pitch\\n- adjust character traits\\n- soften the midpoint\\n- shorten the story target\\n- pause writing\\n- change narration speed\\n\\n### UI-to-Chat\\nA direct UI action should appear in the chat history as a compact structured summary, for example:\\n- “Selected genre: Quest Fantasy”\\n- “Updated target runtime: ~12 minutes”\\n- “Accepted revised beat sheet”\\n\\n### Safety rule\\nThe model can **propose** actions, but a deterministic policy layer should decide whether they are valid in the current session state.\\n\\n## Save The Cat Expectations\\n\\nUse the Save The Cat method as the backbone of planning.\\n\\n- The beat sheet should be explicit, editable, and stored as structured data.\\n- The composition stage should write against the beat sheet and the detailed outline derived from it.\\n- Bedtime adaptation matters: the low points can be emotionally meaningful without becoming harsh or distressing.\\n- The final story should still feel like a transformation story, not just a string of nice scenes.\\n\\n## Genre and Tone Expectations\\n\\nUse the included research docs to seed:\\n- a curated genre list\\n- per-genre tone options\\n- bedtime-suitable arc notes\\n- tone descriptors that can guide UI copy and AI prompting\\n\\nDo **not** label tones with living or copyrighted-author names. Use generic tone labels and descriptors.\\n\\n## Audio Expectations\\n\\nNarration must be treated as a real product feature, not a side demo.\\n\\n- Segment audio generation so it is resumable\\n- Estimate length heuristically before generation\\n- Support voice selection\\n- Support speed controls\\n- Support optional background music\\n- Keep music subordinate to narration clarity\\n- Produce a final compiled audio artifact\\n- Keep intermediate and final artifacts trackable through durable metadata\\n\\n## Export Expectations\\n\\nAt minimum, the user must be able to get:\\n- final story text in the app\\n- final audio in the app\\n- downloadable `.docx`\\n- downloadable final audio file\\n\\n## Developer Experience and Quality Bar\\n\\nFavor:\\n- typed interfaces\\n- boring, explicit service boundaries\\n- migrations\\n- tests around durable state\\n- visible logs\\n- simple scripts for local dev\\n- useful docs\\n\\nAvoid:\\n- stuffing everything into route handlers\\n- leaking secrets to the browser\\n- relying on browser local state as the source of truth\\n- making stage transitions implicit or magical\\n- hiding important state in giant untyped JSON blobs when relational structure is obvious\\n\\n## How to Respond to Each Numbered Prompt\\n\\nFor each numbered prompt in this pack:\\n\\n1. Treat it as the next incremental repo task.\\n2. Preserve prior work unless the new prompt requires refactor.\\n3. Make reasonable local decisions when the prompt leaves room, but stay aligned with this base prompt.\\n4. Prefer small, coherent commits worth reviewing.\\n5. After implementing, report:\\n   - what you changed\\n   - key files touched\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\nprompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.yolopilot.md:842:    prompts/02-vite-react-scaffold.codex.jsonlines:49:{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_25\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,260p' README.md\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"# Storyteller\\n\\nStoryteller is a local-first, full-stack app for creating bedtime stories from first idea to finished text and finished narration audio. The product is session-based: the first meaningful screen is a past-sessions view where a user can resume work in progress, reopen a completed story, or start a new session.\\n\\nThis repository currently contains the first scaffold for that system. The project charter in this README and the supporting docs define the product and architecture that later prompts will implement.\\n\\n## Product Vision\\n\\nThe app should feel like a guided bedtime story studio, not a generic prompt box. A user should be able to:\\n\\n- See past sessions first and continue where they left off.\\n- Collaborate through both chat and direct UI controls.\\n- Move through a structured planning workflow before long-form writing starts.\\n- Watch story composition and audio generation progress in real time.\\n- Return later to read, listen, download, or revise completed work.\\n\\nThe tone of the product should stay calm, readable, and trustworthy. Wonder, mystery, and adventure are welcome, but bedtime suitability is a hard requirement: emotional stakes should resolve safely and the ending should feel restful.\\n\\n## Required Story Workflow\\n\\nThe application is being built around this ordered workflow:\\n\\n1. Genre selection\\n2. Tone selection\\n3. Story setup / free-form brief\\n4. Story pitches\\n5. Character sheet\\n6. Save-the-Cat beat sheet\\n7. Story setup preferences such as word count, runtime, and chapters\\n8. Composition\\n9. Audio configuration and audio generation\\n10. Finalize / read / listen / download\\n\\nEach stage must be durable, resumable, and editable. Chat messages can propose actions in the workflow, and direct UI actions must also be reflected back into the session chat log as compact summaries.\\n\\n## Fixed Technical Decisions\\n\\nThese choices are not optional for this project:\\n\\n- Frontend: React + Vite + TypeScript\\n- Backend: Python + FastAPI\\n- Structured data: PostgreSQL\\n- Blob storage: file-backed GCS emulator in local development\\n- Local orchestration: Docker Compose\\n- Secrets handling: local `secrets.yaml`, never committed to git\\n- AI access: Gemini 3.1 family behind backend-owned adapters and services\\n\\nTwo architectural rules follow from those constraints:\\n\\n- The browser must never hold provider secrets or call Gemini directly.\\n- Long-running story composition and narration generation must be durable background jobs with resumable server-side state.\\n\\nMore detail is in [docs/product-brief.md](/Users/kevin/code/storyteller/docs/product-brief.md) and [docs/architecture-overview.md](/Users/kevin/code/storyteller/docs/architecture-overview.md).\\n\\n## Local Development Expectations\\n\\nThe repo is intended to run locally with Docker Compose. At the current scaffold stage:\\n\\n- `frontend/` contains a Vite React app served on `http://localhost:8566`\\n- `backend/` contains a FastAPI app served on `http://localhost:8565`\\n- `infra/compose/` holds the canonical Compose file for local orchestration\\n- `tools/webapp-qa/` contains the browser automation container used for local UI verification\\n\\nStart the current stack with:\\n\\n```bash\\n./scripts/dev-compose.sh up --build\\n```\\n\\nThe current scaffold does not yet include PostgreSQL or the file-backed GCS emulator, but both are required parts of the target system and will be added through later prompt work. `secrets.yaml` is already gitignored and reserved for local-only credentials.\\n\\n## Repository Shape\\n\\n```text\\n.\\n├── backend/\\n│   ├── app/\\n│   │   ├── main.py\\n│   │   └── worker/\\n│   ├── migrations/\\n│   ├── Dockerfile\\n│   ├── README.md\\n│   └── requirements.txt\\n├── docs/\\n├── frontend/\\n├── infra/\\n│   ├── compose/\\n│   │   └── docker-compose.yml\\n│   └── persistence/\\n├── prompts/\\n├── scripts/\\n├── test-assets/\\n└── tools/\\n    └── webapp-qa/\\n```\\n\\n- `frontend/`: browser client and future TypeScript UI modules\\n- `backend/`: API code, worker home, and migration home\\n- `infra/`: Compose definitions and infrastructure notes\\n- `docs/`: product notes, architecture notes, and future ADRs\\n- `scripts/`: developer entrypoints such as `dev-compose.sh`\\n- `test-assets/`: reusable fixtures for UI, audio, and integration testing\\n- `prompts/`: sequential build prompts and task summaries\\n- `tools/`: local QA and developer tooling\\n\\n## What Success Looks Like\\n\\nA new engineer should be able to open this repository and quickly understand:\\n\\n- the first screen is the past-sessions home for resume, edit, and create flows\\n- the story workflow is staged and ordered\\n- the final product writes stories and generates narration audio durably\\n- the backend owns AI access, long-running jobs, and persistent state\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\nprompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.yolopilot.md:845:    prompts/02-vite-react-scaffold.codex.jsonlines:72:{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_37\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,260p' docs/architecture-overview.md\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"# Architecture Overview\\n\\n## System Picture\\n\\nStoryteller is a full-stack application with a browser client, a backend-owned workflow engine, durable relational state, durable object storage, and resumable background jobs.\\n\\n```text\\nReact + Vite client\\n        |\\n        v\\nFastAPI API layer\\n        |\\n        +--> domain services and policy layer\\n        |         |\\n        |         +--> AI adapters for planning, composition, and narration\\n        |         |\\n        |         +--> repositories for sessions, workflow state, jobs, and event log\\n        |\\n        +--> background workers for composition and audio generation\\n                  |\\n                  +--> PostgreSQL for durable structured state\\n                  +--> file-backed GCS emulator for artifacts and intermediate blobs\\n```\\n\\n## Why Gemini Calls Must Stay on the Backend\\n\\nAll Gemini access belongs on the server side for four reasons:\\n\\n1. Secrets control: provider credentials live in local `secrets.yaml` and must never enter the browser.\\n2. Policy enforcement: chat-to-UI actions and workflow transitions need deterministic server validation before the model can change session state.\\n3. Durable orchestration: prompts, structured outputs, retries, and model selection need to be coordinated with persistent session data and job records.\\n4. Provider flexibility: keeping Gemini behind backend adapters lets the app keep a stable internal interface while model IDs, structured output tactics, or narration providers evolve.\\n\\nThe frontend should only call the backend's API and subscribe to backend-owned progress events.\\n\\n## Why Composition and Audio Must Use Resumable Server-Side Jobs\\n\\nComposition and narration are long-running operations that cannot safely live inside request threads or browser memory. They need server-side jobs because:\\n\\n- a user must be able to refresh the page or return later without losing progress\\n- partial story text and audio segments need durable checkpoints\\n- progress updates need a single source of truth that both chat and UI can observe\\n- retries, cancellation, and resume logic need explicit job state rather than transient in-memory state\\n\\nThe durable model should include job records, segment records, asset metadata, and an event log that can reconstruct session progress after interruption.\\n\\n## Intended Backend Shape\\n\\nThe target backend should separate concerns explicitly:\\n\\n- `api/`: HTTP routes and websocket endpoints\\n- `settings/`: environment and secrets loading\\n- `db/`: database engine, migrations, and persistence setup\\n- `models/`: typed domain models and persistence schemas\\n- `repositories/`: database access for sessions, events, jobs, and assets\\n- `services/`: workflow logic and policy enforcement\\n- `ai/`: Gemini planning, composition, and narration adapters\\n- `worker/`: background job execution for writing and audio\\n- `storage/`: object storage abstraction over the local GCS emulator\\n\\n## Durable Domain Concepts\\n\\nThe system should persist the following concepts as first-class records:\\n\\n- Session\\n- Workflow stage\\n- Event log\\n- Selected genre\\n- Selected tone\\n- Story brief\\n- Pitch batch and selected pitch\\n- Character batch and selected character sheet\\n- Beat sheet\\n- Story setup preferences\\n- Outline or chapter plan\\n- Continuity bible\\n- Composition job and segments\\n- Audio job and segments\\n- Asset metadata\\n\\n## Current Repository State\\n\\nThe repository currently contains an initial scaffold:\\n\\n- `frontend/`: Vite React app\\n- `backend/`: FastAPI app\\n- `backend/migrations/`: reserved home for database schema history\\n- `backend/app/worker/`: reserved home for background job runners\\n- `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml`: local frontend, backend, and browser QA services\\n- `infra/persistence/`: notes for persistent local infrastructure data\\n- `scripts/dev-compose.sh`: convenience entrypoint for the Compose stack\\n- `tools/webapp-qa/`: containerized browser automation support\\n\\nPostgreSQL, the file-backed GCS emulator, background workers, and most domain layers are still planned work. This document records the required direction so later prompts can extend the scaffold without changing the architectural contract.\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\nprompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.yolopilot.md:891:    DEFAULT_CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS = (\nprompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.yolopilot.md:941:        gcs_bucket_name: str\nprompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.yolopilot.md:966:                \"STORYTELLER_CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS\",\nprompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.yolopilot.md:967:                DEFAULT_CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS,\nprompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.yolopilot.md:970:            gcs_bucket_name=os.getenv(\"STORYTELLER_GCS_BUCKET_NAME\", \"\").strip(),\nprompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.yolopilot.md:1038:    from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware\nprompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.yolopilot.md:1084:            CORSMiddleware,\nprompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.yolopilot.md:1145:        assert     ./prompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.codex.jsonlines:60:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_31","type":"agent_message","text":"I have enough context to make the changes without fighting the current scaffold. The implementation plan is:\n\n1. Replace the loose env reader with a validated settings loader that builds a nested config object, discovers `secrets.yaml`, merges `defaults < secrets.yaml < environment`, and emits a single readable error message when required values are missing or malformed.\n2. Keep the current app/service code stable by adapting the health and startup paths to the new settings shape rather than rewriting unrelated modules.\n3. Add a root `secrets.example.yaml` plus docs in the repo and backend README that explain discovery, precedence, Docker Compose behavior, and the fact that Gemini credentials stay backend-only.\n4. Expand backend tests to cover secrets-file loading, env override behavior, missing-config failures, and the startup error path from `python -m app`.\n5. backend/app/settings/config.py\"","aggregated_output":"from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport os\nimport logging\nfrom functools import lru_cache\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any, Mapping, Sequence\n\nimport yaml\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field, SecretStr, ValidationError, field_validator\n\n\nDEFAULT_CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS = (\n    \"http://localhost:8566\",\n    \"http://127.0.0.1:8566\",\n)\nPROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3]\nBACKEND_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]\nDEFAULT_SECRETS_FILE_NAME = \"secrets.yaml\"\nFIELD_HINTS = {\n    \"database.url\": \"STORYTELLER_DATABASE_URL or database.url\",\n    \"gemini.api_key\": \"STORYTELLER_GEMINI_API_KEY or gemini.api_key\",\n    \"gcs.endpoint\": \"STORYTELLER_GCS_ENDPOINT or gcs.endpoint\",\n    \"gcs.project_id\": \"STORYTELLER_GCS_PROJECT_ID or gcs.project_id\",\n    \"gcs.buckets.sessions\": (\n        \"STORYTELLER_GCS_SESSIONS_BUCKET_NAME or gcs.buckets.sessions\"\n    ),\n    \"gcs.buckets.audio\": \"STORYTELLER_GCS_AUDIO_BUCKET_NAME or gcs.buckets.audio\",\n    \"gcs.buckets.exports\": (\n        \"STORYTELLER_GCS_EXPORTS_BUCKET_NAME or gcs.buckets.exports\"\n    ),\n}\n\n\ndef _stringify_error_path(path: Sequence[Any]) -> str:\n    return \".\".join(str(item) for item in path if item != \"__root__\")\n\n\ndef _raise_missing_required_setting(field_path: str) -> None:\n    hint = FIELD_HINTS.get(field_path, field_path)\n    raise ValueError(f\"missing required setting from {hint}\")\n\n\ndef _normalize_string(value: str) -> str:\n    return value.strip()\n\n\ndef _read_bool(value: Any, default: bool | None = None) -> bool | None:\n    if value is None:\n        return default\n\n    if isinstance(value, bool):\n        return value\n\n    normalized = str(value).strip().lower()\n\n    if normalized in {\"1\", \"true\", \"yes\", \"on\"}:\n        return True\n\n    if normalized in {\"0\", \"false\", \"no\", \"off\"}:\n        return False\n\n    raise ValueError(\n        f\"expected a boolean value but received {value!r}; use true/false, yes/no, or 1/0\",\n    )\n\n\ndef _read_int(value: Any, default: int) -> int:\n    if value is None or value == \"\":\n        return default\n\n    return int(value)\n\n\ndef _read_string(value: Any, default: str | None = None) -> str | None:\n    if value is None:\n        return default\n\n    normalized = str(value).strip()\n    return normalized or default\n\n\ndef _read_env_bool(environ: Mapping[str, str], name: str) -> bool | None:\n    try:\n        return _read_bool(environ.get(name))\n    except ValueError as exc:\n        raise SettingsValidationError((f\"{name}: {exc}\",)) from None\n\n\ndef _read_env_int(environ: Mapping[str, str], name: str, default: int) -> int | None:\n    if environ.get(name) is None:\n        return None\n\n    try:\n        return _read_int(environ.get(name), default)\n    except ValueError as exc:\n        raise SettingsValidationError((f\"{name}: {exc}\",)) from None\n\n\ndef _read_env_string_list(\n    environ: Mapping[str, str],\n    name: str,\n    default: tuple[str, ...],\n) -> tuple[str, ...] | None:\n    if environ.get(name) is None:\n        return None\n\n    try:\n        return _read_string_list(environ.get(name), default)\n    except ValueError as exc:\n        raise SettingsValidationError((f\"{name}: {exc}\",)) from None\n\n\ndef _read_string_list(\n    value: Any,\n    default: tuple[str, ...],\n) -> tuple[str, ...]:\n    if value is None or value == \"\":\n        return default\n\n    if isinstance(value, str):\n        items = [item.strip() for item in value.split(\",\")]\n    elif isinstance(value, Sequence):\n        items = [str(item).strip() for item in value]\n    else:\n        raise ValueError(\n            \"expected a comma-separated string or list of strings for CORS origins\",\n        )\n\n    normalized = tuple(item for item in items if item)\n    return normalized or default\n\n\ndef _normalize_api_prefix(value: str) -> str:\n    prefix = value.strip() or \"/api/v1\"\n    return prefix if prefix.startswith(\"/\") else f\"/{prefix}\"\n\n\ndef _deep_merge(base: dict[str, Any], override: Mapping[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    merged = dict(base)\n\n    for key, value in override.items():\n        if isinstance(value, Mapping) and isinstance(merged.get(key), Mapping):\n            merged[key] = _deep_merge(dict(merged[key]), value)\n            continue\n\n        merged[key] = value\n\n    return merged\n\n\ndef _discover_secrets_file(\n    environ: Mapping[str, str],\n    *,\n    cwd: Path | None = None,\n) -> Path | None:\n    explicit = environ.get(\"STORYTELLER_SECRETS_FILE\")\n    search_root = cwd or Path.cwd()\n\n    if explicit is not None:\n        explicit = explicit.strip()\n\n        if not explicit:\n            return None\n\n        candidate = Path(explicit).expanduser()\n\n        if not candidate.is_absolute():\n            candidate = (search_root / candidate).resolve()\n\n        return candidate if candidate.is_file() else None\n\n    candidates = (\n        search_root / DEFAULT_SECRETS_FILE_NAME,\n        BACKEND_ROOT / DEFAULT_SECRETS_FILE_NAME,\n        PROJECT_ROOT / DEFAULT_SECRETS_FILE_NAME,\n    )\n\n    for candidate in candidates:\n        if candidate.is_file():\n            return candidate\n\n    return None\n\n\ndef _load_secrets_file(path: Path | None) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    if path is None:\n        return {}\n\n    try:\n        raw_data = yaml.safe_load(path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n    except OSError as exc:\n        raise SettingsValidationError(\n            (\n                f\"Could not read secrets file at {path}: {exc.strerror or exc}\",\n            ),\n        ) from exc\n    except yaml.YAMLError as exc:\n        raise SettingsValidationError(\n            (\n                f\"Could not parse secrets file at {path}: {exc}\",\n            ),\n        ) from exc\n\n    if raw_data is None:\n        return {}\n\n    if not isinstance(raw_data, dict):\n        raise SettingsValidationError(\n            (\n                f\"Expected a mapping at the root of {path}, found {type(raw_data).__name__}.\",\n            ),\n        )\n\n    return raw_data\n\n\ndef _build_default_payload(environment: str) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    return {\n        \"app_name\": \"Storyteller API\",\n        \"environment\": environment,\n        \"version\": \"0.1.0\",\n        \"host\": \"0.0.0.0\",\n        \"port\": 8565,\n        \"reload\": environment.lower() == \"development\",\n        \"api_v1_prefix\": \"/api/v1\",\n        \"log_level\": \"INFO\",\n        \"cors\": {\n            \"allowed_origins\": DEFAULT_CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS,\n        },\n        \"database\": {},\n        \"gemini\": {},\n        \"gcs\": {\n            \"buckets\": {},\n        },\n        \"feature_flags\": {\n            \"enable_api_docs\": environment.lower() != \"production\",\n            \"enable_audio_generation\": False,\n            \"enable_debug_inspector\": False,\n        },\n    }\n\n\ndef _build_environment_payload(environ: Mapping[str, str]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    legacy_bucket = _read_string(environ.get(\"STORYTELLER_GCS_BUCKET_NAME\"))\n\n    return {\n        \"app_name\": _read_string(environ.get(\"STORYTELLER_APP_NAME\")),\n        \"environment\": _read_string(environ.get(\"STORYTELLER_ENVIRONMENT\")),\n        \"version\": _read_string(environ.get(\"STORYTELLER_VERSION\")),\n        \"host\": _read_string(environ.get(\"STORYTELLER_HOST\")),\n        \"port\": _read_env_int(environ, \"STORYTELLER_PORT\", 8565),\n        \"reload\": _read_env_bool(environ, \"STORYTELLER_RELOAD\"),\n        \"api_v1_prefix\": _read_string(environ.get(\"STORYTELLER_API_V1_PREFIX\")),\n        \"log_level\": _read_string(environ.get(\"STORYTELLER_LOG_LEVEL\")),\n        \"cors\": {\n            \"allowed_origins\": _read_env_string_list(\n                environ,\n                \"STORYTELLER_CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS\",\n                DEFAULT_CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS,\n            ),\n        },\n        \"database\": {\n            \"url\": _read_string(environ.get(\"STORYTELLER_DATABASE_URL\")),\n        },\n        \"gemini\": {\n            \"api_key\": _read_string(environ.get(\"STORYTELLER_GEMINI_API_KEY\")),\n            \"planning_model\": _read_string(\n                environ.get(\"STORYTELLER_GEMINI_PLANNING_MODEL\"),\n            ),\n            \"composition_model\": _read_string(\n                environ.get(\"STORYTELLER_GEMINI_COMPOSITION_MODEL\"),\n            ),\n            \"tts_model\":        \"sessions\": _read_string(\n                    environ.get(\"STORYTELLER_GCS_SESSIONS_BUCKET_NAME\"),\n                    legacy_bucket,\n                ),\n                \"audio\": _read_string(\n                    environ.get(\"STORYTELLER_GCS_AUDIO_BUCKET_NAME\"),\n                    legacy_bucket,\n                ),\n                \"exports\": _read_string(\n key: _prune_none_values(item)\n            for key, item in value.items()\n            if item is not None\n        }\n        return pruned\n\n    return value\n\n\nclass SettingsValidationError(RuntimeError):\n    def __init__(self, issues: Sequence[str]) -> None:\n        self.issues = tuple(issues)\n        super().__init__(self.format_for_cli())\n\n    @classmethod\n    def from_validation_error(cls, exc: ValidationError) -> \"SettingsValidationError\":\n        issues = []\n\n        for error in exc.errors():\n            path = _stringify_error_path(error[\"loc\"])\n            message = error[\"msg\"]\n            issues.append(f\"{path}: {message}\")\n\n        return cls(issues)\n\n    def format_for_cli(self) -> - Time: `2026-03-31T21:52:26-07:00`

### Full Text

    Command: /bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' docs/secrets-and-local-config.md"
    Status: completed
    Exit code: 0
    Output:
    # Secrets and Local Config

    Storyteller loads backend configuration from two sources:

    1. `secrets.yaml` for local-only values
    2. `STORYTELLER_*` environment variables

    Precedence is simple:

    1. built-in defaults
    2. `secrets.yaml`
    3. environment variables

    Environment variables always win over `secrets.yaml`.

    ## File Discovery

    The backend looks for `secrets.yaml` in this order:

    1. the path from `STORYTELLER_SECRETS_FILE`, if that variable is set to a non-empty value
    2. the current working directory
    3. `backend/`
    4. the repository root

    If `STORYTELLER_SECRETS_FILE` is set to an empty string, file discovery is disabled and only environment variables are used.

    The Docker Compose backend service mounts the repository at `/workspace` and sets:

    ```text
    STORYTELLER_SECRETS_FILE=/workspace/secrets.yaml
    ```

    That means a local developer can copy the example file, add a Gemini key, and start the full stack without exposing the key to the browser.

    ## Create a Local File

    Start from the example at the repository root:

    ```bash
    cp secrets.example.yaml secrets.yaml
    ```

    Then replace `gemini.api_key` with a real local key before starting the backend.

    ## Supported Shape

    `secrets.yaml` uses the same nested structure as the backend settings object:

    ```yaml
    database:
      url: postgresql://storyteller:storyteller@postgres:5432/storyteller

    gemini:
      api_key: your-gemini-api-key-here

    gcs:
      endpoint: http://gcs:4443
      project_id: storyteller-local
      public_url: http://localhost:8568
      buckets:
        sessions: storyteller-sessions
        audio: storyteller-audio
        exports: storyteller-exports

    cors:
      allowed_origins:
        - http://localhost:8566

    feature_flags:
      enable_api_docs: true
      enable_audio_generation: false
      enable_debug_inspector: false
    ```

    The required values today are:

    - `database.url`
    - `gemini.api_key`
    - `gcs.endpoint`
    - `gcs.project_id`
    - `gcs.buckets.sessions`
    - `gcs.buckets.audio`
    - `gcs.buckets.exports`

    `gcs.public_url` is optional.

    ## Matching Environment Variables

    The main environment-variable equivalents are:

    - `STORYTELLER_DATABASE_URL`
    - `STORYTELLER_GEMINI_API_KEY`
    - `STORYTELLER_GEMINI_PLANNING_MODEL`
    - `STORYTELLER_GEMINI_COMPOSITION_MODEL`
    - `STORYTELLER_GEMINI_TTS_MODEL`
    - `STORYTELLER_GCS_ENDPOINT`
    - `STORYTELLER_GCS_PROJECT_ID`
    - `STORYTELLER_GCS_PUBLIC_URL`
    - `STORYTELLER_GCS_SESSIONS_BUCKET_NAME`
    - `STORYTELLER_GCS_AUDIO_BUCKET_NAME`
    - `STORYTELLER_GCS_EXPORTS_BUCKET_NAME`
    - `STORYTELLER_CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS`
    - `STORYTELLER_FEATURE_ENABLE_API_DOCS`
    - `STORYTELLER_FEATURE_ENABLE_AUDIO_GENERATION`
    - `STORYTELLER_FEATURE_ENABLE_DEBUG_INSPECTOR`

    For compatibility with the earlier scaffold, `STORYTELLER_GCS_BUCKET_NAME` still works as a fallback and fills all three bucket names when the more specific variables are absent.

    ## Validation Behavior

    Startup fails fast when required values are missing or malformed. The backend reports a short error list instead of a long Python traceback when launched with:

    ```bash
    python -m app
    ```

    The frontend never receives the Gemini API key. The browser only talks to the FastAPI backend, and all Gemini access stays in backend-owned services and adapters.

    name: storyteller

    services:
      postgres:
        image: postgres:16-alpine
        restart: unless-stopped
        environment:
          POSTGRES_DB: storyteller
          POSTGRES_USER: storyteller
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: storyteller
        ports:

        volumes:
          - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
        healthcheck:
          test:
            - CMD-SHELL
            - pg_isready -U storyteller -d storyteller
          interval: 5s
          timeout: 5s
          retries: 20
          start_period: 5s

      gcs:
        image: fsouza/fake-gcs-server:1.54.0
        restart: unless-stopped
        command:
          - -scheme
          - http
          - -backend
          - filesystem
          - -filesystem-root
          - /data
          - -public-host
          - localhost:8568
          - -external-url
          - http://localhost:8568
        ports:
          - "8568:4443"
        volumes:
          - gcs_data:/data
        healthcheck:
          test:
            - CMD-SHELL
            - wget -q -O - http://127.0.0.1:4443/storage/v1/b >/dev/null
          interval: 5s
          timeout: 5s
          retries: 20
          start_period: 5s

      backend:
        build:
          context: ../../backend
        depends_on:
          postgres:
            condition: service_healthy
          gcs:
            condition: service_healthy
        working_dir: /app
        volumes:
          - ../../backend:/app
          - ../../:/workspace:ro
        environment:
          PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE: "1"
          PYTHONUNBUFFERED: "1"
          STORYTELLER_SECRETS_FILE: /workspace/secrets.yaml
          STORYTELLER_ENVIRONMENT: development
          STORYTELLER_DATABASE_URL: postgresql://storyteller:storyteller@postgres:5432/storyteller
          STORYTELLER_GCS_ENDPOINT: http://gcs:4443
          STORYTELLER_GCS_PROJECT_ID: storyteller-local
          STORYTELLER_GCS_PUBLIC_URL: http://localhost:8568
          STORYTELLER_GCS_SESSIONS_BUCKET_NAME: storyteller-sessions
          STORYTELLER_GCS_AUDIO_BUCKET_NAME: storyteller-audio
          STORYTELLER_GCS_EXPORTS_BUCKET_NAME: storyteller-exports
        ports:
          - "8565:8565"
        command: python -m app
        healthcheck:
          test:
            - CMD
            - python
            - -c
            - |
              import sys, urllib.request
              urllib.request.urlopen("http://127.0.0.1:8565/health")
              sys.exit(0)
          interval: 5s
          timeout: 5s
          retries: 20
          start_period: 5s

      frontend:
        build:
          context: ../../frontend
        depends_on:
          backend:
            condition: service_healthy
        working_dir: /app
        environment:
          CHOKIDAR_USEPOLLING: "true"
          VITE_DEV_PROXY_TARGET: http://backend:8565
        volumes:
          - ../../frontend:/app
          - frontend_node_modules:/app/node_modules
        ports:
          - "8566:8566"
        command: npm run dev -- --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8566
        healthcheck:
          test:
            - CMD
            - node
            - -e
            - |
              require("http").get("http://127.0.0.1:8566", (response) => {
                process.exit(response.statusCode === 200 ? 0 : 1);
              }).on("error", () => process.exit(1));
          interval: 5s
          timeout: 5s
          retries: 20
          start_period: 10s

      browser:
        build:
          context: ../../tools/webapp-qa
        depends_on:
          frontend:
            condition: service_healthy
        working_dir: /workspace/tools/webapp-qa
        environment:
          PUPPETEER_EXECUTABLE_PATH: /usr/bin/chromium
          TARGET_BASE_URL: http://frontend:8566
        volumes:
          - ../../:/workspace
          - webapp_qa_node_modules:/workspace/tools/webapp-qa/node_modules
        command: sleep infinity

    volumes:
      frontend_node_modules:
      postgres_data:
      gcs_data:
      webapp_qa_node_modules:

    # Scripts

    This directory holds small developer entrypoints and helper scripts.

    Current scripts:

    - `dev-compose.sh`: wraps the canonical Compose file under `infra/compose/`

    Keep scripts narrow in scope and readable enough that engineers can trust them at a glance.

    Common usage:

    - `./scripts/dev-compose.sh up --build` to start or rebuild the local stack
    - `./scripts/dev-compose.sh down` to stop while preserving Postgres and GCS volumes
    - `./scripts/dev-compose.sh down --volumes` to intentionally reset persistent local infrastructure data

    set -euo pipefail

    ROOT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"

    docker compose -f "${ROOT_DIR}/infra/compose/docker-compose.yml" "$@"

    Status: completed
    Exit code: 0
    Output:
    # Persistence Notes

    Use this directory for notes about local durable data as infrastructure services are added.

    Examples that belong here:

    - where Docker volumes store PostgreSQL state in development
    - how the file-backed GCS emulator persists blobs across restarts
    - backup, reset, and cleanup guidance for local persistent data

    This directory is for documentation and conventions, not checked-in database dumps or generated runtime data.

    Current Compose-backed persistent volumes:

    - `storyteller_postgres_data`: mounted into `/var/lib/postgresql/data`
    - `storyteller_gcs_data`: mounted into `/data` for the local fake GCS server

    Safe cleanup guidance:

    - `./scripts/dev-compose.sh down` stops containers and preserves both volumes
    - `./scripts/dev-compose.sh down --volumes` removes the containers and both named volumes
    - avoid deleting volumes unless you want to wipe the local database and object storage state

### Full Text

    Command: /bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' README.md"
    Status: completed
    Exit code: 0
    Output:
    # Storyteller

    Storyteller is a local-first, full-stack app for creating bedtime stories from first idea to finished text and finished narration audio. The product is session-based: the first meaningful screen is a past-sessions view where a user can resume work in progress, reopen a completed story, or start a new session.

    This repository currently contains the first scaffold for that system. The project charter in this README and the supporting docs define the product and architecture that later prompts will implement.

    ## Product Vision

    The app should feel like a guided bedtime story studio, not a generic prompt box. A user should be able to:

    - See past sessions first and continue where they left off.
    - Collaborate through both chat and direct UI controls.
    - Move through a structured planning workflow before long-form writing starts.
    - Watch story composition and audio generation progress in real time.
    - Return later to read, listen, download, or revise completed work.

    The tone of the product should stay calm, readable, and trustworthy. Wonder, mystery, and adventure are welcome, but bedtime suitability is a hard requirement: emotional stakes should resolve safely and the ending should feel restful.

    ## Required Story Workflow

    The application is being built around this ordered workflow:

    1. Genre selection
    2. Tone selection
    3. Story setup / free-form brief
    4. Story pitches
    5. Character sheet
    6. Save-the-Cat beat sheet
    7. Story setup preferences such as word count, runtime, and chapters
    8. Composition
    9. Audio configuration and audio generation
    10. Finalize / read / listen / download

    Each stage must be durable, resumable, and editable. Chat messages can propose actions in the workflow, and direct UI actions must also be reflected back into the session chat log as compact summaries.

    ## Fixed Technical Decisions

    These choices are not optional for this project:

    - Frontend: React + Vite + TypeScript
    - Backend: Python + FastAPI
    - Structured data: PostgreSQL
    - Blob storage: file-backed GCS emulator in local development
    - Local orchestration: Docker Compose
    - Secrets handling: local `secrets.yaml`, never committed to git
    - AI access: Gemini 3.1 family behind backend-owned adapters and services

    Two architectural rules follow from those constraints:

    - The browser must never hold provider secrets or call Gemini directly.
    - Long-running story composition and narration generation must be durable background jobs with resumable server-side state.

    More detail is in [docs/product-brief.md](/Users/kevin/code/storyteller/docs/product-brief.md) and [docs/architecture-overview.md](/Users/kevin/code/storyteller/docs/architecture-overview.md).

    ## Local Development Expectations

    The repo is intended to run locally with Docker Compose. At the current scaffold stage:

    - `frontend/` contains a Vite React app served on `http://localhost:8566`
    - `backend/` contains a FastAPI app served on `http://localhost:8565`
    - `infra/compose/` holds the canonical Compose file for local orchestration
    - `tools/webapp-qa/` contains the browser automation container used for local UI verification

    Start the current stack with:

    ```bash
    cp secrets.example.yaml secrets.yaml
    ./scripts/dev-compose.sh up --build
    ```

    The local Compose stack now includes:

    - `frontend` on `http://localhost:8566`
    - `backend` on `http://localhost:8565`
    - `postgres` on `localhost:8567`
    - `gcs` on `http://localhost:8568`

    `secrets.yaml` is already gitignored and reserved for local-only credentials. Copy `secrets.example.yaml` to `secrets.yaml`, add a Gemini API key, and keep the file local. The compose file uses development defaults for Postgres and the GCS emulator, while the backend loads secrets from the repo-root `secrets.yaml` at container startup.

    ## Docker Compose Local Stack

    Use the repository wrapper so Docker Compose always targets the canonical file under `infra/compose/`:

    ```bash
    ./scripts/dev-compose.sh up --build
    ```

    Stop the stack without deleting persisted data:

    ```bash
    ./scripts/dev-compose.sh down
    ```

    Reset the local database and file-backed object storage only when you intentionally want a clean slate:

    ```bash
    ./scripts/dev-compose.sh down --volumes
    ```

    Persistent data lives in named Docker volumes:

    - `storyteller_postgres_data` for PostgreSQL
    - `storyteller_gcs_data` for the file-backed GCS emulator

    The backend receives the local infrastructure coordinates through environment variables in Compose:

    - `STORYTELLER_DATABASE_URL=postgresql://storyteller:storyteller@postgres:5432/storyteller`
    - `STORYTELLER_GCS_ENDPOINT=http://gcs:4443`
    - `STORYTELLER_GCS_SESSIONS_BUCKET_NAME=storyteller-sessions`
    - `STORYTELLER_GCS_AUDIO_BUCKET_NAME=storyteller-audio`
    - `STORYTELLER_GCS_EXPORTS_BUCKET_NAME=storyteller-exports`
    - `STORYTELLER_GCS_PROJECT_ID=storyteller-local`
    - `STORYTELLER_GCS_PUBLIC_URL=http://localhost:8568`

    The backend settings layer merges defaults, `secrets.yaml`, and environment variables in that order. More detail is in [docs/secrets-and-local-config.md](/Users/kevin/code/storyteller/docs/secrets-and-local-config.md).

    ## Repository Shape

    ```text
    .
    ├── backend/
    │   ├── app/
    │   │   ├── api/
    │   │   ├── db/
    │   │   ├── models/
    │   │   ├── services/
    │   │   ├── settings/
    │   │   ├── main.py
    │   │   └── worker/
    │   ├── tests/
    │   ├── migrations/
    │   ├── Dockerfile
    │   ├── README.md
    │   └── requirements.txt
    ├── docs/
    ├── frontend/
    ├── infra/
    │   ├── compose/
    │   │   └── docker-compose.yml
    │   └── persistence/
    ├── prompts/
    ├── scripts/
    ├── test-assets/
    └── tools/
        └── webapp-qa/
    ```

    - `frontend/`: browser client and TypeScript UI foundation
    - `backend/`: FastAPI API code, settings, services, tests, worker home, and migration home
    - `infra/`: Compose definitions and infrastructure notes
    - `docs/`: product notes, architecture notes, and future ADRs
    - `scripts/`: developer entrypoints such as `dev-compose.sh`
    - `test-assets/`: reusable fixtures for UI, audio, and integration testing
    - `prompts/`: sequential build prompts and task summaries
    - `tools/`: local QA and developer tooling

    ## What Success Looks Like

    A new engineer should be able to open this repository and quickly understand:

    - the first screen is the past-sessions home for resume, edit, and create flows
    - the story workflow is staged and ordered
    - the final product writes stories and generates narration audio durably
    - the backend owns AI access, long-running jobs, and persistent state

    @@ -75,7 +76,7 @@ The local Compose stack now includes:

    -`secrets.yaml` is already gitignored and reserved for local-only credentials. Copy `secrets.example.yaml` to `secrets.yaml`, add a Gemini API key, and keep the file local. The compose file uses development defaults for Postgres and the GCS emulator, while the backend loads secrets from the repo-root `secrets.yaml` at container startup.
    +`secrets.yaml` is already gitignored and reserved for local-only credentials. Copy `secrets.example.yaml` to `secrets.yaml`, add a Gemini API key, and keep the file local. `./scripts/install-git-hooks.sh` enables the repo-managed pre-commit hook that blocks accidental commits of `secrets.yaml`, `.env` files, and obvious live key material. The compose file uses development defaults for Postgres and the GCS emulator, while the backend loads secrets from the repo-root `secrets.yaml` at container startup.
 
     ## Docker Compose Local Stack
 
    diff --git a/docs/secrets-and-local-config.md b/docs/secrets-and-local-config.md
    index 48b53d2..d2dbbb7 100644
    --- a/docs/secrets-and-local-config.md
    +++ b/docs/secrets-and-local-config.md
    @@ -13,6 +13,20 @@ Precedence is simple:
 
     Environment variables always win over `secrets.yaml`.
 
    +## Commit Hygiene
    +
    +`secrets.yaml` is the live local credentials file. It must never be committed, copied into tracked examples, or pasted into checked-in docs. The repository keeps exactly one tracked template for that shape: `secrets.example.yaml`.
    +
    +When contributors need a safe sample file, it should use an example-style name such as:
    +
    +- `secrets.example.yaml`
    +- `.env.example`
    +- `.env.local.example`
    +
    +Tracked examples must contain placeholders only. Real credentials belong in local-only files such as `secrets.yaml` or in shell environment variables that stay outside git.
    +
    +The repository also ignores `.env`, `.env.*`, and reserved local persistence paths under `infra/persistence/` so machine-specific config and runtime data do not pollute `git status`.
    +
     ## File Discovery
 
     The backend looks for `secrets.yaml` in this order:
    @@ -42,6 +56,14 @@ cp secrets.example.yaml secrets.yaml
 
     Then replace `gemini.api_key` with a real local key before starting the backend.
 
    +To enable the repo-managed pre-commit guard for this clone, run:
    +
    +```bash
    +./scripts/install-git-hooks.sh
    +```
    +
    +The hook blocks commits that stage `secrets.yaml`, `.env` files, or obvious Gemini/private-key material.
    +
     ## Supported Shape
 
     `secrets.yaml` uses the same nested structure as the backend settings object:
    diff --git a/infra/persistence/README.md b/infra/persistence/README.md
    index bd39259..3eaada0 100644
    --- a/infra/persistence/README.md
    +++ b/infra/persistence/README.md
    @@ -10,6 +10,15 @@ Examples that belong here:
 
     This directory is for documentation and conventions, not checked-in database dumps or generated runtime data.
 
    +If future local infrastructure work needs host-mounted runtime state instead of named Docker volumes, keep that data under one of these reserved ignored paths:
    +
    +- `infra/persistence/runtime/`
    +- `infra/persistence/postgres-data/`
    +- `infra/persistence/gcs-data/`
    +- `infra/persistence/local/`
    +
    +Those directories are gitignored so local persistence experiments and recovered data do not pollute commits.
    +
     Current Compose-backed persistent volumes:
 
     - `storyteller_postgres_data`: mounted into `/var/lib/postgresql/data`
    diff --git a/scripts/README.md b/scripts/README.md
    index a6f5cdb..311ea14 100644
    --- a/scripts/README.md
    +++ b/scripts/README.md
    @@ -4,7 +4,9 @@ This directory holds small developer entrypoints and helper scripts.
 
     Current scripts:
 
    +- `check-secret-hygiene.sh`: blocks staged local-only config files and obvious secret material
     - `dev-compose.sh`: wraps the canonical Compose file under `infra/compose/`
    +- `install-git-hooks.sh`: points `core.hooksPath` at the repo-managed `.githooks/` directory
 
     Keep scripts narrow in scope and readable enough that engineers can trust them at a glance.
 
    @@ -13,3 +15,4 @@ Common usage:
     - `./scripts/dev-compose.sh up --build` to start or rebuild the local stack
     - `./scripts/dev-compose.sh down` to stop while preserving Postgres and GCS volumes
     - `./scripts/dev-compose.sh down --volumes` to intentionally reset persistent local infrastructure data
    +- `./scripts/install-git-hooks.sh` to enable the lightweight pre-commit secret guard for this clone

### Full Text

    Command: /bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba docs/secrets-and-local-config.md | sed -n '1,260p'"
    Status: completed
    Exit code: 0
    Output:
    1	# Secrets and Local Config
         2	
         3	Storyteller loads backend configuration from two sources:
         4	
         5	1. `secrets.yaml` for local-only values
         6	2. `STORYTELLER_*` environment variables
         7	
         8	Precedence is simple:
         9	
        10	1. built-in defaults
        11	2. `secrets.yaml`
        12	3. environment variables
        13	
        14	Environment variables always win over `secrets.yaml`.
        15	
        16	## Commit Hygiene
        17	
        18	`secrets.yaml` is the live local credentials file. It must never be committed, copied into tracked examples, or pasted into checked-in docs. The repository keeps exactly one tracked template for that shape: `secrets.example.yaml`.
        19	
        20	When contributors need a safe sample file, it should use an example-style name such as:
        21	
        22	- `secrets.example.yaml`
        23	- `.env.example`
        24	- `.env.local.example`
        25	
        26	Tracked examples must contain placeholders only. Real credentials belong in local-only files such as `secrets.yaml` or in shell environment variables that stay outside git.
        27	
        28	The repository also ignores `.env`, `.env.*`, and reserved local persistence paths under `infra/persistence/` so machine-specific config and runtime data do not pollute `git status`.
        29	
        30	## File Discovery
        31	
        32	The backend looks for `secrets.yaml` in this order:
        33	
        34	1. the path from `STORYTELLER_SECRETS_FILE`, if that variable is set to a non-empty value
        35	2. the current working directory
        36	3. `backend/`
        37	4. the repository root
        38	
        39	If `STORYTELLER_SECRETS_FILE` is set to an empty string, file discovery is disabled and only environment variables are used.
        40	
        41	The Docker Compose backend service mounts the repository at `/workspace` and sets:
        42	
        43	```text
        44	STORYTELLER_SECRETS_FILE=/workspace/secrets.yaml
        45	```
        46	
        47	That means a local developer can copy the example file, add a Gemini key, and start the full stack without exposing the key to the browser.
        48	
        49	## Create a Local File
        50	
        51	Start from the example at the repository root:
        52	
        53	```bash
        54	cp secrets.example.yaml secrets.yaml
        55	```
        56	
        57	Then replace `gemini.api_key` with a real local key before starting the backend.
        58	
        59	To enable the repo-managed pre-commit guard for this clone, run:
        60	
        61	```bash
        62	./scripts/install-git-hooks.sh
        63	```
        64	
        65	The hook blocks commits that stage `secrets.yaml`, `.env` files, or obvious Gemini/private-key material.
        66	
        67	## Supported Shape
        68	
        69	`secrets.yaml` uses the same nested structure as the backend settings object:
        70	
        71	```yaml
        72	database:
        73	  url: postgresql://storyteller:storyteller@postgres:5432/storyteller
        74	
        75	gemini:
        76	  api_key: your-gemini-api-key-here
        77	
        78	gcs:
        79	  endpoint: http://gcs:4443
        80	  project_id: storyteller-local
        81	  public_url: http://localhost:8568
        82	  buckets:
        83	    sessions: storyteller-sessions
        84	    audio: storyteller-audio
        85	    exports: storyteller-exports
        86	
        87	cors:
        88	  allowed_origins:
        89	    - http://localhost:8566
        90	
        91	feature_flags:
        92	  enable_api_docs: true
        93	  enable_audio_generation: false
        94	  enable_debug_inspector: false
        95	```
        96	
        97	The required values today are:
        98	
        99	- `database.url`
       100	- `gemini.api_key`
       101	- `gcs.endpoint`
       102	- `gcs.project_id`
       103	- `gcs.buckets.sessions`
       104	- `gcs.buckets.audio`
       105	- `gcs.buckets.exports`
       106	
       107	`gcs.public_url` is optional.
       108	
       109	## Matching Environment Variables
       110	
       111	The main environment-variable equivalents are:
       112	
       113	- `STORYTELLER_DATABASE_URL`
       114	- `STORYTELLER_GEMINI_API_KEY`
       115	- `STORYTELLER_GEMINI_PLANNING_MODEL`
       116	- `STORYTELLER_GEMINI_COMPOSITION_MODEL`
       117	- `STORYTELLER_GEMINI_TTS_MODEL`
       118	- `STORYTELLER_GCS_ENDPOINT`
       119	- `STORYTELLER_GCS_PROJECT_ID`
       120	- `STORYTELLER_GCS_PUBLIC_URL`
       121	- `STORYTELLER_GCS_SESSIONS_BUCKET_NAME`
       122	- `STORYTELLER_GCS_AUDIO_BUCKET_NAME`
       123	- `STORYTELLER_GCS_EXPORTS_BUCKET_NAME`
       124	- `STORYTELLER_CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS`
       125	- `STORYTELLER_FEATURE_ENABLE_API_DOCS`
       126	- `STORYTELLER_FEATURE_ENABLE_AUDIO_GENERATION`
       127	- `STORYTELLER_FEATURE_ENABLE_DEBUG_INSPECTOR`
       128	
       129	For compatibility with the earlier scaffold, `STORYTELLER_GCS_BUCKET_NAME` still works as a fallback and fills all three bucket names when the more specific variables are absent.
       130	
       131	## Validation Behavior
       132	
       133	Startup fails fast when required values are missing or malformed. The backend reports a short error list instead of a long Python traceback when launched with:
       134	
       135	```bash
       136	python -m app
       137	```
       138	
       139	The frontend never receives the Gemini API key. The browser only talks to the FastAPI backend, and all Gemini access stays in backend-owned services and adapters.

    1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
         2	set -euo pipefail
         3	
         4	ROOT_DIR="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
         5	cd "${ROOT_DIR}"
         6	
         7	staged_paths=()
         8	while IFS= read -r path; do
         9	  staged_paths+=("${path}")
        10	done < <(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR)
        11	
        12	if [[ "${#staged_paths[@]}" -eq 0 ]]; then
        13	  exit 0
        14	fi
        15	
        16	blocked_paths=()
        17	
        18	for path in "${staged_paths[@]}"; do
        19	  case "${path}" in
        20	    secrets.yaml|*/secrets.yaml)
        21	      blocked_paths+=("${path}")
        22	      ;;
        23	    .env|*/.env|.env.*|*/.env.*|.envrc|*/.envrc)
        24	      case "${path}" in
        25	        .env.example|*/.env.example|.env.sample|*/.env.sample|.env.template|*/.env.template|.env.*.example|*/.env.*.example|.env.*.sample|*/.env.*.sample|.env.*.template|*/.env.*.template)
        26	          ;;
        27	        *)
        28	          blocked_paths+=("${path}")
        29	          ;;
        30	      esac
        31	      ;;
        32	  esac
        33	done
        34	
        35	if [[ "${#blocked_paths[@]}" -gt 0 ]]; then
        36	  printf 'Secret hygiene check failed.\n' >&2
        37	  printf 'Refusing to commit local-only config files:\n' >&2
        38	  printf '  %s\n' "${blocked_paths[@]}" >&2
        39	  printf '\nUse tracked example files such as secrets.example.yaml or .env.example for placeholders.\n' >&2
        40	  exit 1
        41	fi
        42	
        43	staged_patch="$(git diff --cached --unified=0 --no-color -- .)"
        44	added_lines="$(printf '%s\n' "${staged_patch}" | rg '^\+' | rg -v '^\+\+\+' || true)"
        45	
        46	if [[ -z "${added_lines}" ]]; then
        47	  exit 0
        48	fi
        49	
        50	if private_key_hits="$(printf '%s\n' "${added_lines}" | rg -n 'BEGIN [A-Z ]*PRIVATE KEY' || true)"; [[ -n "${private_key_hits}" ]]; then
        51	  printf 'Secret hygiene check failed.\n' >&2
        52	  printf 'Detected staged private key material:\n%s\n' "${private_key_hits}" >&2
        53	  exit 1
        54	fi
        55	
        56	if gemini_key_hits="$(printf '%s\n' "${added_lines}" | rg -n 'AIza[0-9A-Za-z_-]{20,}' || true)"; [[ -n "${gemini_key_hits}" ]]; then
        57	  printf 'Secret hygiene check failed.\n' >&2
        58	  printf 'Detected staged Gemini or Google API key material:\n%s\n' "${gemini_key_hits}" >&2
        59	  exit 1
        60	fi
        61	
        62	if assigned_secret_hits="$(
        63	  printf '%s\n' "${added_lines}" \
        64	    | rg -n '^\+.*(STORYTELLER_GEMINI_API_KEY|GEMINI_API_KEY|api_key)[[:space:]]*[:=][[:space:]]*.+$' \
        65	    | rg -vi '(example|sample|template|placeholder|changeme|replace|dummy|test-key|your-|<your)' \
        66	    || true
        67	)"; [[ -n "${assigned_secret_hits}" ]]; then
        68	  printf 'Secret hygiene check failed.\n' >&2
        69	  printf 'Detected staged secret assignment lines:\n%s\n' "${assigned_secret_hits}" >&2
        70	  printf '\nMove live credentials into secrets.yaml or local environment variables instead.\n' >&2
        71	  exit 1
        72	fi

    ---
         1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
         2	set -euo pipefail
         3	
         4	ROOT_DIR="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
         5	
         6	exec "${ROOT_DIR}/scripts/check-secret-hygiene.sh"

    ---
        60	- `backend/` contains a FastAPI app served on `http://localhost:8565`
        61	- `infra/compose/` holds the canonical Compose file for local orchestration
        62	- `tools/webapp-qa/` contains the browser automation container used for local UI verification
        63	
        64	Start the current stack with:
        65	
        66	```bash
        67	cp secrets.example.yaml secrets.yaml
        68	./scripts/install-git-hooks.sh
        69	./scripts/dev-compose.sh up --build
        70	```
        71	
        72	The local Compose stack now includes:
        73	
        74	- `frontend` on `http://localhost:8566`
        75	- `backend` on `http://localhost:8565`
        76	- `postgres` on `localhost:8567`
        77	- `gcs` on `http://localhost:8568`
        78	
        79	`secrets.yaml` is already gitignored and reserved for local-only credentials. Copy `secrets.example.yaml` to `secrets.yaml`, add a Gemini API key, and keep the file local. `./scripts/install-git-hooks.sh` enables the repo-managed pre-commit hook that blocks accidental commits of `secrets.yaml`, `.env` files, and obvious live key material. The compose file uses development defaults for Postgres and the GCS emulator, while the backend loads secrets from the repo-root `secrets.yaml` at container startup.
        80	
        81	## Docker Compose Local Stack
        82	
        83	Use the repository wrapper so Docker Compose always targets the canonical file under `infra/compose/`:
        84	
        85	```bash
        86	./scripts/dev-compose.sh up --build
        87	```
        88	
        89	Stop the stack without deleting persisted data:
        90	
        91	```bash
        92	./scripts/dev-compose.sh down
        93	```
        94	
        95	Reset the local database and file-backed object storage only when you intentionally want a clean slate:

    ---
         1	# Scripts
         2	
         3	This directory holds small developer entrypoints and helper scripts.
         4	
         5	Current scripts:
         6	
         7	- `check-secret-hygiene.sh`: blocks staged local-only config files and obvious secret material
         8	- `dev-compose.sh`: wraps the canonical Compose file under `infra/compose/`
         9	- `install-git-hooks.sh`: points `core.hooksPath` at the repo-managed `.githooks/` directory
        10	
        11	Keep scripts narrow in scope and readable enough that engineers can trust them at a glance.
        12	
        13	Common usage:
        14	
        15	- `./scripts/dev-compose.sh up --build` to start or rebuild the local stack
        16	- `./scripts/dev-compose.sh down` to stop while preserving Postgres and GCS volumes
        17	- `./scripts/dev-compose.sh down --volumes` to intentionally reset persistent local infrastructure data
        18	- `./scripts/install-git-hooks.sh` to enable the lightweight pre-commit secret guard for this clone

    ---
         1	# Persistence Notes
         2	
         3	Use this directory for notes about local durable data as infrastructure services are added.
         4	
         5	Examples that belong here:
         6	
         7	- where Docker volumes store PostgreSQL state in development
         8	- how the file-backed GCS emulator persists blobs across restarts
         9	- backup, reset, and cleanup guidance for local persistent data
        10	
        11	This directory is for documentation and conventions, not checked-in database dumps or generated runtime data.
        12	
        13	If future local infrastructure work needs host-mounted runtime state instead of named Docker volumes, keep that data under one of these reserved ignored paths:
        14	
        15	- `infra/persistence/runtime/`
        16	- `infra/persistence/postgres-data/`
        17	- `infra/persistence/gcs-data/`
        18	- `infra/persistence/local/`
        19	
        20	Those directories are gitignored so local persistence experiments and recovered data do not pollute commits.
        21	
        22	Current Compose-backed persistent volumes:
        23	
        24	- `storyteller_postgres_data`: mounted into `/var/lib/postgresql/data`
        25	- `storyteller_gcs_data`: mounted into `/data` for the local fake GCS server
        26	
        27	Safe cleanup guidance:
        28	
        29	- `./scripts/dev-compose.sh down` stops containers and preserves both volumes
        30	- `./scripts/dev-compose.sh down --volumes` removes the containers and both named volumes
        31	- avoid deleting volumes unless you want to wipe the local database and object storage state
